headline: Oil Prices Steady, S&P 500 Dips As Reserve Release Offsets Mine Fears
summary: Oil prices remained in a holding pattern on Wednesday morning as the International Energy Agency announced a record release of petroleum reserves by member countries to avoid a supply crunch.  The S&P 500 fell slightly, despite a strong earnings report fom Oracle.  The IEA said its 32 member nations agreed to jointly release 400 million barrels of oil.

raw.headline: Oil Prices Steady, S&P 500 Dips As Reserve Release Offsets Mine Fears

headline: Oil Is All About Iran Right Now But Chevron Focuses On Venezuela
summary: While U.S. oil prices held above $80 per barrel early Wednesday amid the ongoing war in Iran and the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, Chevron and Shell are focused on securing significant oil production agreements in Venezuela.  Reuters, citing five sources, reported late Tuesday that Chevron and Shell are close to finalizing the first big oil production deals with Venezuela since the U.S., in early January, captured President Nicolas Maduro.  The report from Reuters comes about a month after Venezuela reformed its oil laws, granting foreign companies the ability to operate, export and sell Venezuelan oil even if they are minority partners of state-owned oil company PDVSA.

headline: Is Baker Hughes Stock Outperforming the S&P 500?
summary: As Baker Hughes has considerably outperformed the S&P 500 recently, analysts remain highly optimistic about the stock’s prospects.

headline: Oil Prices Rise, S&P 500 Futures Fall As Reserve Plans Offset Mine Fears
summary: Oil prices remained in a holding pattern early Wednesday as plans for a joint release of emergency petroleum reserves moved forward offset fears that the Strait of Hormuz could remain closed indefinitely amid concerns that Iranian vessels are laying mines.  S&P 500 futures rose slightly, helped by a strong earnings report from Oracle.  The International Energy Agency has recommended the release of a record 400 million barrels of oil by its 32-member nations, according to The Wall Street Journal.

raw.headline: Oil Prices Rise, S&P 500 Futures Fall As Reserve Plans Offset Mine Fears

S&P 500 futures rose slightly, helped by a strong earnings report from Oracle.  The International Energy Agency has recommended the release of a record 400 million barrels of oil by its 32-member nations, according to The Wall Street Journal.

headline: How The Baker Hughes (BKR) Story Is Shifting As Analysts Rework Their Targets
summary: The analyst narrative around Baker Hughes is shifting, with the average fair value estimate edging from about US$60.80 to roughly US$61.38 per share. Recent research has clustered around upward price target revisions, while a small number of cuts highlight the debate over how much recent sector strength already reflects optimistic scenarios. As you read on, you will see how these moves fit together and what to watch to keep up with the evolving story around Baker Hughes. Analyst Price Targets...
